results.takeaway: Recent basic science research advances understanding of molecular and cellular pathways in oral and craniomaxillofacial tissue repair, paving the way for new cell-based and molecular treatment strategies for bone and soft tissue regeneration in oral and craniomaxillofacial surgery.

results.title: Bioengineering strategies for regeneration of craniofacial bone: a review of emerging technologies.
results.author: B. Ward
results.takeaway: Tissue engineering, combining engineering, biology, and medicine, offers promising solutions for restoring and maintaining normal function of oral and craniofacial tissues damaged by trauma or disease.

results.title: Role of hormones in bone remodeling in the craniofacial complex: A review
results.author: Erin Grinde Tunheim
results.journal: Journal of Oral Biology and Craniofacial Research
results.takeaway: Hormones play a complex role in bone remodeling in the craniofacial complex, potentially aiding in treating bone-related diseases.

results.title: Small molecule-mediated regenerative engineering for craniofacial and dentoalveolar bone
results.author: J. Mitchell
results.takeaway: Small molecule-based compounds show potential for cranial and periodontal bone regeneration, offering a promising approach for complex medical cases involving cancer, cranioplasty, and traumatic injury.

results.title: The distinctive jaw and alveolar bone regeneration.
results.author: S. Akintoye
results.takeaway: The jaw bone has unique developmental, biological, and clinical features compared to other non-craniofacial bones, making it a crucial bone for facial reconstruction and facial development.

results.title: Nanomaterial-integrated injectable hydrogels for craniofacial bone reconstruction
results.author: Yong Xia
results.takeaway: Nanomaterial-integrated injectable hydrogels offer a shape-adaptive alternative for noninvasive craniofacial bone reconstruction, offering enhanced mechanical properties and therapeutic benefits.

results.title: Developmental pathways of periodontal tissue regeneration: Developmental diversities of tooth morphogenesis do also map capacity of periodontal tissue regeneration?
results.author: U. Ripamonti
results.takeaway: TGF- may play a role in inducing tissue morphogenesis across animal phyla, potentially affecting periodontal tissue regeneration in vertebrates.

results.title: Regeneration of Jaw Joint Cartilage in Adult Zebrafish
results.author: Joanna Smeeton
results.journal: Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology
results.takeaway: Adult zebrafish can regenerate damaged jaw joint cartilage, demonstrating their potential as a model for studying joint regeneration in humans.

results.title: Limb and tail loss and regeneration in the Nauta Mushroomtongue Salamander (Bolitoglossa altamazonica) in the Madre de Dios Region, Peru
results.author: J. Oakley
results.takeaway: Nauta mushroomtongue salamanders (Bolitoglossa altamazonica) can regenerate limbs and tails, demonstrating unique regenerative capabilities in amphibians.

results.title: Positional Memory in Vertebrate Regeneration: A Century's Insights from the Salamander Limb.
results.author: L. Otsuki
results.journal: Cold Spring Harbor perspectives in biology
results.takeaway: Positional memory in vertebrate regeneration, as demonstrated in the salamander limb model, could lead to improved tissue repair and tissue engineering for therapeutic purposes.

results.title: To regenerate or not to regenerate: Vertebrate model organisms of regeneration-competency and -incompetency
results.author: Can Aztekin
results.journal: Wound repair and regeneration : official publication of the Wound Healing Society [and] the European Tissue Repair Society
results.takeaway: Studying model organisms with varying regenerative abilities can help uncover factors contributing to regeneration-incompetency in mammals and test functional restoration strategies.

results.title: Orofacial Muscles: Embryonic Development and Regeneration after Injury
results.author: D. H. Rosero Salazar
results.takeaway: Orofacial muscles have lower regenerative ability and more fibrosis than other skeletal muscles, requiring specific approaches to stimulate regeneration and reduce fibrosis for improved surgical treatment of defects.

results.title: Harnessing the Therapeutic Potential of Cell Secretomes and Extracellular Vesicles for Craniofacial Regenerative Applications.
results.author: S. Shanbhag
results.takeaway: Cell-free therapies, particularly extracellular vesicles, show potential for craniofacial regeneration, with preclinical data showing a clear adjunctive benefit with biomaterial scaffolds for bone and periodontal regeneration.

results.title: Craniofacial Tissue Engineering
results.author: Weibo Zhang
results.journal: Cold Spring Harbor perspectives in medicine
results.takeaway: Craniofacial tissue engineering aims to provide rapid, effective, and aesthetic reconstruction of craniofacial defects, addressing both structural and psychosocial issues.

results.title: Recent update on craniofacial tissue engineering
results.author: Aala’a Emara
results.takeaway: Tissue engineering advancements show potential in treating craniofacial defects, with 3D printing enabling patient-specific reconstruction and enhancing post-operative esthetic results.

results.title: Ligament injury in adult zebrafish triggers ECM remodeling and cell dedifferentiation for scar-free regeneration
results.author: Troy Anderson
results.takeaway: Adult zebrafish can heal scar-free ligaments, highlighting the importance of immune-mesenchyme cross-talk in ECM remodeling and regeneration.

results.title: Re-examining jaw regeneration in urodeles: what have we learnt?
results.author: Patrizia Ferretti
results.journal: The International journal of developmental biology
results.takeaway: Urodele amphibians can regenerate their upper and lower jaws, but more research is needed to understand the molecular mechanisms underlying this process.

results.title: Tail regeneration and other phenomena of wound healing and tissue restoration in lizards
results.author: Kathy Jacyniak
results.takeaway: Lizards exhibit unique wound healing and regeneration abilities, including the ability to regrow their entire tails and other tissues after injury, offering potential for biomedical sciences and regenerative medicine.

results.title: Invited Letter. Organ regeneration occurs in vertebrates with aquatic-related life cycles including metamorphosis and was lost during land transition.
results.author: Lorenzo Alibardi
results.takeaway: Organ regeneration in amphibians and fish is influenced by their aquatic life cycle and evolutionary history, while in terrestrial vertebrates, wound healing and scarring are limited.

results.title: Appendage Regeneration in Vertebrates: What Makes This Possible?
results.author: Valentina Daponte
results.takeaway: Understanding the similarities and differences in appendage regeneration in lizards, salamanders, and zebrafish can guide effective regenerative strategies in mammals.

50.state: Craniofacial and jaw regeneration is an active frontier — Wnt signalling and periosteal stem cells (EGR1, ALPL+PDGFD+) drive craniofacial bone repair, and adult newts regrow jaws with bone, cartilage and teeth (a 2026 study). The 1905 lizard-jaw result anticipates a field now pursued for human reconstruction.

facets.q: Can experimentally induced colour changes be inherited across generations (Lamarckian or epigenetic inheritance)?
Is there evidence for transgenerational inheritance of acquired pigmentation?

results.title: The Midwife Toad Challenge After (Half) a Century
results.author: J. Arntzen
results.takeaway: Paul Kammerer's claim that environmentally induced character states are carried over to the next generation remains unconfirmed, but his experiment on fire salamander color pattern change has been confirmed.

results.title: Role of environmentally induced epigenetic transgenerational inheritance in evolutionary biology: Unified Evolution Theory
results.author: M. Skinner
results.takeaway: Environmental epigenetics can directly influence phenotypic variation, genetic variation, inheritance, and adaptation, providing a more unified theory of evolutionary biology.

results.title: Can epigenetics translate environmental cues into phenotypes?
results.author: P. Norouzitallab
results.journal: The Science of the total environment
results.takeaway: Epigenetics has revolutionized our understanding of how organisms adapt to environmental cues and pass acquired phenotypes on to future generations, challenging the gene-based view of inheritance.

results.title: Molecular mechanisms of transgenerational epigenetic inheritance
results.author: Maximilian H. Fitz-James
results.takeaway: Epigenetic information, such as DNA methylation, histone modifications, and non-coding RNAs, can be inherited across generations in organisms, contributing to phenotypic variation and evolution.

results.title: Mechanisms of transgenerational epigenetic inheritance: lessons from animal model organisms.
results.author: Flavio Santilli
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance in animal models involves environmental sensing by the germline, which influences phenotypic traits in subsequent generations, with implications for human populations.

results.title: Inheritance of environment-induced phenotypic changes through epigenetic mechanisms
results.author: Yukiko Tando
results.takeaway: Parental environmental factors can cause epigenetic changes that alter the phenotypes of descendants through various mechanisms, including DNA methylation, histone modification, and small RNAs via metabolites.

results.title: Epigenetic-Genetic Coupling and Understanding the Molecular and Cellular Basis of Lamarckian Inheritance.
results.author: R. Lindley
results.takeaway: Lamarckian inheritance of somatically acquired adaptations involves stable, DNA-integrated inheritance, requiring specific population dynamics and sustained environmental stimuli for consolidation.

results.title: Lamarck rises from his grave: parental environment‐induced epigenetic inheritance in model organisms and humans
results.author: Yan Wang
results.takeaway: Parental environment-induced epigenetic alterations can transmit across generations in various organisms, with sex-specific effects, and require further investigation for better understanding of gene-environment interactions.

results.title: Molecular mechanisms of epigenetic inheritance: Possible evolutionary implications
results.author: P. Sarkies
results.takeaway: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance may provide short-term adaptation advantages, contribute to species evolution, and influence the evolution of human cancer.

results.title: Generational stability of epigenetic transgenerational inheritance facilitates adaptation and evolution
results.author: A. Korolenko
results.takeaway: Epigenetics and epigenetic inheritance significantly influence phenotypic variation in various species, correlated with evolutionary change and requiring integration into evolutionary theory for a better understanding of biology.

results.title: Lamarckism and epigenetic inheritance: a clarification
results.author: Laurent Loison
results.takeaway: Lamarckism and epigenetic inheritance are distinct concepts, with epigenetic mechanisms evolving as products of natural selection, and inheritance of acquired characters being an emergent feature of evolution.

results.title: Evolutionary consequences of epigenetic inheritance
results.author: M. Lind
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance, which involves non-genetic transmission of modified phenotypes across generations, has significant evolutionary consequences, particularly in terms of adaptation and the effects of paternal inheritance.

results.title: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance during plant evolution and breeding.
results.author: Shuai Cao
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance in plants, influenced by environmental and genomic factors, can be harnessed to improve crop yield and resilience in the face of environmental challenges.

results.title: Is Lamarckism returning to evolutionary biology?
results.author: A. Berber
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance of acquired epigenetic marks does not represent a return to Lamarckism in evolutionary biology.

results.title: Heredity determined by the environment: Lamarckian ideas in modern molecular biology.
results.author: O. N. Tikhodeyev
results.journal: The Science of the total environment
results.takeaway: Inheritance of acquired characteristics (IAC) is a special case of genotype-environment interactions, with most mechanisms being epigenetic, and some types potentially being adaptive elements of natural selection.

results.title: Epigenetic inheritance and evolution: a historian's perspective
results.author: Laurent Loison
results.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance may potentiate neglected mechanisms like the Baldwin effect and genetic assimilation, potentially strengthening the causal relationship between adaptability and adaptation in evolutionary theory.

results.title: From Environmental Epigenetics to the Inheritance of Acquired Traits: A Historian and Molecular Perspective on an Unnecessary Lamarckian Explanation
results.author: M. Mandrioli
results.takeaway: Epigenetic inheritance as a Lamarckian process is incorrect from a historical and scientific perspective, and current adaptation mechanisms remain firmly within the Darwinian paradigm.

results.title: Environmental Epigenetics and a Unified Theory of the Molecular Aspects of Evolution: A Neo-Lamarckian Concept that Facilitates Neo-Darwinian Evolution
results.author: M. Skinner
results.takeaway: Environmental epigenetics can significantly impact natural selection by directly altering phenotypic variation and facilitating neo-Darwinian evolution.

results.title: The inheritance of acquired epigenetic variations.
results.author: E. Jablonka
results.takeaway: Acquired epigenetic variations can influence gene expression in the next generation, potentially affecting adaptive evolution and causing a type of "Lamarckian" inheritance.

results.title: Elusive inheritance: Transgenerational effects and epigenetic inheritance in human environmental disease
results.author: S. Martos
results.journal: Progress in biophysics and molecular biology
results.takeaway: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance of environmental changes in mammals is rare, and its role in human environmental diseases remains elusive due to the complexity of the mammalian lifecycle and the difficulty of establishing a role for epigenetic inheritance.

results.title: Transgenerational effect of drug-mediated inhibition of LSD1 on eye pigment expression in Drosophila
results.author: S. Hoyer-Fender
results.takeaway: Feeding flies with the LSD1 inhibitor Tranylcypromine increases eye pigment expression and can be transmitted to subsequent generations, suggesting transgenerational epigenetic inheritance.

results.title: Transgenerational inheritance of acquired epigenetic signatures at CpG islands in mice.
results.author: Yuta Takahashi
results.takeaway: DNA methylation of promoter-associated CpG islands can be transmitted from parents to their offspring in mice, potentially impacting our understanding of evolutionary biology and non-genetically inherited human diseases.

results.title: Transgenerational inheritance: how impacts to the epigenetic and genetic information of parents affect offspring health.
results.author: M. J. Xavier
results.takeaway: Environmental factors, ageing, diet, and toxicant exposure can alter the epigenetic composition of parents, potentially affecting offspring's health and well-being.

results.title: “Acquired Characters”
results.author: J. Cunningham
results.takeaway: Acquired characters can be inherited, as demonstrated by the absence of pigment in a flat fish after thousands of generations exposed to light.

results.title: Interchromosomal contacts between regulatory regions trigger stable transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in Drosophila
results.author: Maximilian H. Fitz-James
results.takeaway: Chromatin contacts between regulatory regions in Drosophila mediate stable transgenerational epigenetic inheritance, allowing hemizygosity at one locus to establish epigenetic memory at another distant locus.

results.title: Calling the question: what is mammalian transgenerational epigenetic inheritance?
results.author: Hasan Khatib
results.takeaway: This study proposes five fundamental criteria for establishing transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in mammals, highlighting the need for a unified scientific consensus on its requirements.

results.title: A critical view on transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in humans
results.author: B. Horsthemke
results.takeaway: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in humans is controversial due to the complexity of studying genetic, ecological, and cultural inheritance.

results.title: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in mammals: how good is the evidence?
results.author: Sanne D. Otterdijk
results.takeaway: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in mammals is possible, but its impact on early development remains unclear, and its relationship with intrauterine exposure to environmental stressors remains unclear.

results.title: A systematic scientometric review of paternal inheritance of acquired metabolic traits
results.author: L. Crisóstomo
results.takeaway: Paternally transmitted acquired metabolic traits can impact the health of non-exposed descendants, with potential implications for non-communicable diseases.

results.title: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in farm animals: How substantial is the evidence?
results.author: H. Khatib
results.takeaway: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance in farm animals is controversial, with evidence from plants and fruit flies, but not well-established in mammals.

results.title: Transgenerational epigenetic inheritance: a critical perspective
results.author: A. Bird
results.takeaway: The evidence for environmentally induced epigenetic inheritance in mammals remains inconclusive, with genome sequencing strengthening the causal relationship between genotypes and organismal characteristics.

results.title: Negative evidence on the transgenerational inheritance of defense priming in Arabidopsis thaliana
results.author: Se-Hun Yun
results.takeaway: The transgenerational inheritance of defense priming in Arabidopsis thaliana is not supported, suggesting that previous observations on this issue should be reassessed.

results.title: Human and animal evidence of potential transgenerational inheritance of health effects: An evidence map and state-of-the-science evaluation.
results.author: V. Walker
results.takeaway: Early life exposures can potentially cause adverse health effects in offspring, but current evidence is limited due to risk of bias, few studies, and heterogeneity in exposures and outcomes.

results.title: A Theoretical Hypothesis on How Immune Cells May Transmit Acquired Traits: A Macrophage–piRNA Pathway for Transgenerational Inheritance
results.author: Douglas M. Ruden

results.title: Transgenerational Inheritance of Environmentally Induced Epigenetic Alterations during Mammalian Development
results.author: L. Legoff
results.takeaway: Environmental factors can induce parental alterations and affect offspring phenotypes through gametic epigenetic inheritance, influencing disease risk assessment.

results.title: Genome-wide association study reveals genetic architecture and evolution of human retinal pigmentation
results.author: Jian Yuan
results.takeaway: Deep learning-based deep learning framework DeepGRP identifies 42 genetic signals in retinal pigmentation, revealing polygenic adaptation in Europeans driven by snow-reflected light at high latitudes and a polygenic risk score for myopia.

results.title: Transgenerational Epigenetic Inheritance of Cold Adaptation in Rice: Evidence for Neo-Lamarckian Concepts.
results.author: Pingxian Zhang

113.state: Kammerer's inheritance-of-acquired-colour claim remains unconfirmed, but the broader phenomenon — environmental epigenetic inheritance via DNA methylation, histone marks and non-coding RNAs — is a major active field. The specific Lamarckian claim is doubted; the general question is live.

facets.q: Does temperature determine sex in reptiles (temperature-dependent sex determination)?
How do environmental conditions influence the germ line and sex determination in reptiles?

results.title: Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination in Vertebrates
results.author: N. Valenzuela
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles and many fish is determined not by the chromosomes they inherit but by the temperature at which incubation takes place.

results.title: Climate change and temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles.
results.author: F. Janzen
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
results.takeaway: Climate change may negatively impact reptile populations, with even modest increases in mean temperature (2 degrees C) potentially skewing sex ratios and potentially eliminating male offspring production.

results.title: Temperature-dependent sex determination.
results.author: N. Valenzuela
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) is a unique mechanism in reptiles, affecting sex determination during incubation and potentially impacting sex ratios over time.

results.title: Understanding the role of environmental temperature on sex determination through comparative studies in reptiles and amphibians.
results.author: H. Akashi
results.journal: Journal of experimental zoology. Part A, Ecological and integrative physiology
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) in reptiles and amphibians influences sexual differentiation pathways, resulting in distinct primary sex ratios.

results.title: Temperature-dependent sex determination is mediated by pSTAT3 repression of Kdm6b
results.author: C. Weber
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles is mediated by pSTAT3 repression of Kdm6b, with pSTAT3 phosphorylation at warmer temperatures and calcium influx acting as a temperature-sensitive regulator of STAT3 activation.

results.title: Sex Determination in Reptiles
results.author: J. Bull
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) is common in turtles and lizards, and may interfere with the evolution of sex chromosomes and live-bearing, potentially affecting their ancestry.

results.title: Two transcriptionally distinct pathways drive female development in a reptile with both genetic and temperature dependent sex determination
results.author: S. Whiteley
results.takeaway: Temperature determines sex in Pogona vitticeps reptiles, with early developmental cascades differing between genetically driven and thermally driven females, and genes playing a role in temperature sex reversal.

results.title: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles: proximate mechanisms, ultimate outcomes, and practical applications.
results.author: David Crews
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles occurs through gene activation and endocrine microenvironment modification, with practical applications in conservation and monitoring of environmental contaminants.

results.title: Temperature-dependent sex determination : a mechanistic approach
results.author: T. Wibbels
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) in reptiles may be influenced by endogenous estrogen production, but the precise physiological basis remains unclear.

results.title: The ecology and evolution of temperature‐dependent reaction norms for sex determination in reptiles: a mechanistic conceptual model
results.author: N. Pezaro
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles can be linked to environmental variation, potentially affecting their adaptive capacity to climate change and affecting conservation and management strategies.

results.title: The histone demethylase KDM6B regulates temperature-dependent sex determination in a turtle species
results.author: C. Ge
results.takeaway: The histone demethylase KDM6B plays a crucial role in temperature-dependent sex determination in red-eared slider turtles, promoting male-to-female sex reversal when disrupted at 26°C.

results.title: Viviparous Reptile Regarded to Have Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination Has Old XY Chromosomes
results.author: Paola Cornejo-Páramo
results.takeaway: Climate can predict sex determination systems in reptiles, and viviparity is strictly associated with sex chromosomes, as seen in water skinks Eulamprus tympanum and Eulamprus heatwolei.

results.title: Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination in Reptiles
results.author: Valentine
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) is a widespread nongenetic process in reptiles, affecting sex development in embryos affected by environmental factors like temperature.

results.title: Temperature‐Dependent Sex Determination in Sea Turtles in the Context of Climate Change: Uncovering the Adaptive Significance
results.author: P. Santidrián Tomillo
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) in sea turtles may provide an adaptive advantage in maintaining populations under warming climates by coadapting between early survival and sex ratios.

results.title: The adaptive significance of temperature-dependent sex determination in a reptile
results.author: Daniel A. Warner
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in short-lived lizards maximizes the fitness of each sex, supporting the adaptive significance of environmental sex determination in amniote vertebrates.

results.title: How does temperature determine sex?
results.author: A. Georges
results.takeaway: Temperature-responsive epigenetic regulation in reptiles reveals a molecular mechanism that allows temperature to profoundly influence sexual development and sex determination in reptiles.

results.title: Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination and Contemporary Climate Change
results.author: N. Mitchell
results.takeaway: Reptiles with temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) are at risk from rapid global warming, and management should focus on neutralizing biases and promoting adaptive processes to aid species persistence and buy time for research.

results.title: "The Adaptive Significance of Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination in a Reptile" (2008), by Daniel Warner and Richard Shine
results.author: Karla T Moeller
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) evolved in the Jacky dragon as an adaptation to fluctuating environmental temperatures, supporting the Charnov-Bull model.

results.title: Temperature-Dependent Sex Determination in Crocodilians and Climate Challenges
results.author: B. Schilling-Tóth
results.journal: Animals : an Open Access Journal from MDPI
results.takeaway: Crocodilians' sex determination is influenced by the temperature during egg development, and climate change may impact their sex distribution and reproductive success.

results.title: Environmental Sex Determination Mechanisms in Reptiles
results.author: Horacio Merchant-Larios
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles varies among species, with estrogen levels potentially playing a role, and environmental factors like temperature and hormones also playing a role in sex determination.

results.title: Higher temperatures directly increase germ cell number, promoting feminization of red-eared slider turtles.
results.author: B. Tezak
results.takeaway: Higher temperatures directly increase primordial germ cell number in red-eared slider turtle embryos, promoting feminization and potentially improving female reproductive potential.

results.title: Sex Determination and Ovarian Development in Reptiles and Amphibians: From Genetic Pathways to Environmental Influences
results.author: Christopher R. Smaga
results.takeaway: Reptiles and amphibians show diverse reproductive strategies and ovarian development, influenced by genetic pathways and environmental factors, such as temperature and anthropogenic chemicals.

results.title: Environmental Sex Determination in Reptiles: Ecology, Evolution, and Experimental Design
results.author: F. Janzen
results.takeaway: Temperature-dependent sex determination (TSD) is present in three major reptilian lineages, with potential adaptive significance and implications for sex determination in other vertebrates.

results.title: Gonadal Differentiation in Reptiles Exhibiting Environmental Sex Determination
results.author: S. Kohno
results.takeaway: Reptiles exhibit temperature-dependent and genetic sex determination systems, which have emerged de novo, and understanding molecular information on gonadal morphogenesis and function could help understand vertebrate evolution and environmental health.

results.title: Do covariances between maternal behavior and embryonic physiology drive sex-ratio evolution under environmental sex determination?
results.author: F. Janzen
results.takeaway: Maternal oviposition behavior and embryonic thermal sensitivity of sex determination may evolve independently in reptiles with temperature-dependent sex determination, potentially impacting their response to rapidly changing environments.

results.title: Gonadal sex and temperature independently influence germ cell differentiation and meiotic progression in Trachemys scripta
results.author: Talia Hatkevich
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
results.takeaway: Temperature directly affects germ cell sexual differentiation and meiotic progression in the red-eared slider turtle, suggesting that not all meiosis events are under somatic control.

results.title: The Embryos of Turtles Can Influence Their Own Sexual Destinies.
results.author: Yin-Zi Ye
results.takeaway: Behavioral thermoregulation by turtle embryos can buffer sex ratio shifts caused by global warming, expanding the range of conditions where equal numbers of male and female offspring can be produced.

results.title: Transcriptomic thermal plasticity underlying gonadal development in a turtle with ZZ/ZW sex chromosomes despite canalized genotypic sex determination
results.author: Thea B. Gessler
results.takeaway: Transcriptional plasticity in developing gonads persists for over 145 Myr in a turtle with genotypic sex determination, offering potential for adaptive shifts in developmental programming.

results.title: Context-dependent thermolability of sex determination in a lacertid lizard with heteromorphic sex chromosomes
results.author: Alexander Hansson
results.takeaway: Warmer temperatures can override genetic sex determination in a lacertid lizard, leading to overproduction of daughters, but this effect is inconsistent and may be a trait under selection.

results.title: HerpSexDet: the herpetological database of sex determination and sex reversal
results.author: E. Nemesházi
results.takeaway: HerpSexDet is a comprehensive database on herpetological sex determination, containing data on genetic, temperature-dependent sex determination, and sex reversal for 192 amphibian and 697 reptile species.

results.title: Cellular calcium and redox regulation: the mediator of vertebrate environmental sex determination?
results.author: M. Castelli
results.takeaway: Environmental cues in reptiles and fish are sensed by conserved calcium and redox elements, which then activate signal transduction pathways or influence epigenetic processes, driving differential expression of sex genes.

results.title: The ends of a continuum: genetic and temperature‐dependent sex determination in reptiles
results.author: S. Sarre
results.takeaway: Reptile sex determination should be seen as a continuum, with genetic and environmental mechanisms coexisting and interacting to determine sex phenotypes, rather than being fundamentally different.

results.title: Genotype-by-environment interactive effects and conflict solving during gonadal sex differentiation of pejerrey Odontesthes bonariensis, a fish with dual genotypic/environmental sex determination
results.author: Chenyan Wu
results.takeaway: In the pejerrey fish, sex determination is easier for males due to the combination of ovarian formation and testis formation, which allows for more accurate sex determination under conflicting genotypic and environmental signals.

results.title: Sex reversal triggers the rapid transition from genetic to temperature-dependent sex
results.author: C. Holleley
results.takeaway: Sex reversal in reptiles, such as the Australian bearded dragon, can rapidly transition from genetic to temperature-dependent sex determination, potentially affecting adaptation to global climate change.

136.state: Temperature-dependent sex determination in reptiles is a major field: TSD is being dissected mechanistically (pSTAT3-Kdm6b) and climate-warming threats to sex ratios are a live concern. Przibram's environment-and-sex framing maps directly onto modern environmental sex determination.

facets.q: What is the molecular basis of positional memory in amphibian limb regeneration?
Why does an edge-restricted limb defect regenerate a heteromorphic abnormal structure?

results.title: Molecular basis of positional memory in limb regeneration
results.author: L. Otsuki
results.takeaway: Positional memory in limb regeneration in axolotls is stabilized by a positive-feedback loop, and can be reprogrammed more easily from anterior to posterior direction, impacting tissue engineering.

results.title: Positional Memory in Vertebrate Regeneration: A Century's Insights from the Salamander Limb.
results.author: L. Otsuki
results.journal: Cold Spring Harbor perspectives in biology
results.takeaway: Positional memory in vertebrate regeneration, as demonstrated in the salamander limb model, could lead to improved tissue repair and tissue engineering for therapeutic purposes.

results.title: Molecular basis for positional memory and its reprogrammability in limb regeneration
results.author: L. Otsuki
results.takeaway: A positive feedback mechanism in limb regeneration allows for the reprogramming of positional memory, allowing for more easily altered limb identities in tissue engineering.

results.title: Use of retinoids to analyze the cellular basis of positional memory in regenerating amphibian limbs.
results.author: D. Stocum
results.journal: Biochemistry and cell biology = Biochimie et biologie cellulaire
results.takeaway: Retinoids, such as retinoic acid, can modify positional memory in amphibian limb regeneration, potentially through a cellular RA-binding protein.

results.title: A chromatin code for limb segment identity in axolotl limb regeneration.
results.author: Akane Kawaguchi
results.takeaway: Axolotl limb connective tissue cells use segment-specific levels of histone H3K27me3 as an intrinsic segment information code, supporting an ancient, conserved regeneration program.

results.title: Hierarchical pattern formation during amphibian limb regeneration
results.author: Warren A. Vieira
results.takeaway: Hierarchical pattern formation in amphibian limb regeneration involves four basic steps: cell memory, communication, induction of molecular signaling centers, and specialized cell types interpreting signals to restore the limb.

results.title: Lmx1b activation in axolotl limb regeneration
results.author: Sakiya Yamamoto
results.takeaway: Lmx1b activation in axolotl limb regeneration is triggered by nerve presence, enabling positional memory to be reactivated and triggering limb regeneration.

results.title: Stability and plasticity of positional memory during limb regeneration in Ambystoma mexicanum
results.author: R. Iwata
results.takeaway: Positional memories on anteroposterior and dorsoventral axes are relatively stable during limb regeneration in Ambystoma mexicanum, even though cellular differentiation is reprogrammed.

results.title: The Use of Retinoids to Explore the Cellular and Molecular Basis of Positional Memory in Regenerating Amphibian Limbs
results.author: D. Stocum
results.takeaway: Retinoids can help explore the cellular and molecular basis of positional memory in amphibian limb regeneration, potentially revealing intrinsic or extrinsic mechanisms.

results.title: ECM-mediated positional cues are able to induce pattern, but not new positional information, during axolotl limb regeneration
results.author: Warren A. Vieira
results.takeaway: ECM-mediated positional cues play a role in Type-2 communication during axolotl limb regeneration, influencing cell arrangement without new positional information.

results.title: Genetic reprogramming of positional memory in a regenerating appendage
results.author: Ying-Ting Wang
results.takeaway: Positional information in regenerating tissues is malleable, with zebrafish tailfins retaining memory of their new size when disrupted during a 48-hour window of regeneration.

results.title: In preprints: cellular memory - the tension between old and new identities in the blastema.
results.author: Benjamin Tajer
results.takeaway: Hand2 expression in the posterior limb of axolotl salamanders serves as a seat of memory for posteriorizing activity during limb development and regeneration, potentially enforcing a fixed posterior identity during regeneration.

results.title: Transcriptional Correlates of Proximal-Distal Identity and Regeneration Timing in Axolotl Limbs
results.author: S. R. Voss
results.journal: Comparative biochemistry and physiology. Toxicology & pharmacology : CBP
results.takeaway: Positional memory in axolotl limbs is linked to gene expression patterns, which explain the similar regeneration time for proximal and distal amputations.

results.title: Positional information in axolotl and mouse limb extracellular matrix is mediated via heparan sulfate and fibroblast growth factor during limb regeneration in the axolotl (Ambystoma mexicanum)
results.author: A. Phan
results.takeaway: Positional information in limb regeneration is mediated by heparan sulfate and fibroblast growth factor, establishing a crucial link between salamander regeneration and mammals.

results.title: Limb blastema cell: A stem cell for morphological regeneration
results.author: K. Tamura
results.takeaway: Limb blastema cells play a crucial role in morphological regeneration, providing insights into creating three-dimensional morphology during regeneration processes.

results.title: The regeneration of positional information in the amphibian limb.
results.author: M. Maden
results.takeaway: The amphibian limb regeneration process follows five simple rules, with the cell surface playing a crucial role in positional information regeneration and development.

results.title: Transcriptomic, proteomic, and metabolomic landscape of positional memory in the caudal fin of zebrafish
results.author: Jeremy S. Rabinowitz
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences
results.takeaway: Positional memory in zebrafish caudal fins relies on complex overlapping expression patterns for hundreds of molecules involved in diverse cellular functions, aiding future studies on regeneration.

results.title: Cells keep a memory of their tissue origin during axolotl limb regeneration
results.author: M. Kragl
results.takeaway: Limb regeneration in axolotls can be achieved without complete dedifferentiation to a pluripotent state, with restricted progenitor cells and cell-type-specific positional identity.

results.title: Modeling proximalisation in axolotl limb regeneration
results.author: Hernan Arce
results.takeaway: Prod1 and Tig1 promote proximalization in axolotl limb regeneration, guiding cells to acquire positional identity and guide limb regeneration.

results.title: [Regulation of the spatial organization of the regenerating extremity in amphibians].
results.author: D. Stocum
results.takeaway: Retinoic acid modulates positional memory in amphibian limb regeneration blastema, affecting the cellular basis of this memory and affecting gene expression in regenerating limbs.

results.title: Centronuclear myopathies.
results.author: N. Romero
results.takeaway: Mutations in SPEG, encoding SPEG, cause a CNM phenotype due to its interaction with MTM1, and individuals with SPEG mutations may also have dilated cardiomyopathy.

results.title: Amphibian Limb Regeneration: Rebuilding a Complex Structure
results.author: J. Brockes
results.journal: American Association for the Advancement of Science
results.takeaway: Urodele amphibians can regenerate their limbs by local formation of a mesenchymal growth zone or blastema, potentially respecifying proximodistal identity in newt blastemal cells through retinoic acid receptor signaling.

results.title: Beyond Membrane Remodeling: Organelle Crosstalk and Convergent Pathology in Centronuclear Myopathy
results.author: Bana Abolibdeh
results.takeaway: Centronuclear myopathy (CNM) is a disorder involving impaired organelle communication and structural crosstalk, requiring a comprehensive, multi-target approach for full phenotypic rescue across affected tissues.

results.title: Perturb-and-Restore: Simulation-driven Structural Augmentation Framework for Imbalance Chromosomal Anomaly Detection
results.author: Yilan Zhang

results.title: Integration failure of regenerated limb tissue is associated with incongruencies in positional information in the Mexican axolotl
results.author: Warren A. Vieira
results.journal: Frontiers in Cell and Developmental Biology
results.takeaway: Integrating regenerated limb tissue in the Mexican axolotl is hindered by mismatched expression of limb patterning genes, with anterior positional information more abundant at the limb base.

results.title: In preprints: retinoic acid degradation and tissue stiffness coordinate axolotl limb regeneration.
results.author: W. Masselink

results.title: Antiviral Treatment for Congenital Cytomegalovirus Infection in Extremely Preterm Newborn: A Case Report and Literature Review
results.author: Giovanni Boscarino

results.title: Regenerative failure of intrahepatic biliary cells in Alagille syndrome rescued by elevated Jagged/Notch/Sox9 signaling
results.author: Chengjian Zhao
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
results.takeaway: Elevated Jagged/Notch/Sox9 signaling can rescue liver regeneration failure in Alagille syndrome, potentially offering a potential therapeutic avenue for this disorder.

results.title: Two tissue-resident progenitor lineages drive distinct phenotypes of heterotopic ossification
results.author: Devaveena Dey
results.takeaway: Distinct phenotypes of heterotopic ossification in FOP are driven by cell-autonomous effects of dysregulated ACVR1 signaling in nonoverlapping tissue-resident progenitor pools, which may be addressed by systemic therapy or modulating injury-mediated factors.

results.title: Corneal-Committed Cells Restore the Stem Cell Pool and Tissue Boundary following Injury.
results.author: Waseem Nasser
results.takeaway: Committed corneal cells can dedifferentiate, repopulate the stem cell pool, and correctly re-form the tissue boundary after injury, provided the stroma remains intact.

results.title: Serial genomic inversions induce tissue-specific architectural stripes, gene misexpression and congenital malformations
results.author: K. Kraft
results.takeaway: Balanced chromosomal rearrangements can cause tissue-specific architectural stripes, gene misexpression, and congenital malformations by disrupting promoter-enhancer contacts and affecting gene regulation.

results.title: ANO5 ensures trafficking of annexins in wounded myofibers
results.author: Steven J. Foltz
results.takeaway: ANO5 plays a novel, scramblase-independent role in muscle repair, disrupting annexin coordination during repair in Ano5 knockout mice.

results.title: Long-Term, Stochastic Editing of Regenerative Anatomy via Targeting Endogenous Bioelectric Gradients
results.author: Fallon Durant
results.takeaway: Brief perturbation of endogenous bioelectrical networks can permanently alter regenerating planarian anatomy, resulting in a constant ratio of double-headed to normal morphology.

results.title: BIN1 reduction ameliorates DNM2-related Charcot–Marie–Tooth neuropathy
results.author: Marie Goret
results.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America
results.takeaway: BIN1 reduction can improve motor performance and peripheral nerve defects in mice with DNM2-related Charcot-Marie-Tooth neuropathy, offering a potential therapeutic strategy.

results.title: BMP signaling is essential for sustaining proximo-distal progression in regenerating axolotl limbs
results.author: É. Vincent
results.takeaway: BMP signaling is essential for proper patterning and progression in regenerating axolotl limbs, demonstrating regeneration as a progressive process like development.

results.title: Limb-girdle muscular dystrophy type R2 manifesting as fetal growth restriction in a woman with two pregnancies: a case report
results.author: Kaiyu Fu

results.title: Next-Generation Sequencing to Diagnose Muscular Dystrophy, Rhabdomyolysis, and HyperCKemia
results.author: Lily Wu
results.journal: Canadian Journal of Neurological Sciences / Journal Canadien des Sciences Neurologiques
results.takeaway: NGS is a useful tool for accurately diagnosing neuromuscular disorders, identifying pathogenic and likely pathogenic variants in 36.09% of patients.

results.title: Epimorphic regeneration of the mouse digit tip is finite
results.author: Connor P. Dolan
results.takeaway: Epimorphic regeneration in mammals is finite, due to the exhaustion of the blastema cell source's proliferation and differentiation capacity.

results.title: Morphological patterns in limb deficiencies and duplications.
results.author: Ronan O'Rahilly

6.state: Positional memory is central to limb-regeneration research: axolotl studies show it is stabilized by a positive-feedback loop, reprogrammable by retinoic acid, and encoded in segment-specific H3K27me3. Why an edge-restricted defect yields a heteromorphic structure is exactly this positional-information problem.

facets.q: Does the first cleavage plane predict the dorsoventral or median axis of the amphibian embryo?
How is the body axis specified relative to the egg's cytoplasmic organization in amphibians?

results.title: The first cleavage furrow demarcates the dorsal-ventral axis in Xenopus embryos.
results.author: S. L. Klein
results.takeaway: The first cleavage furrow in Xenopus embryos demarcates the dorsal-ventral axis, indicating the dorsal side of the embryo when it bisects the region of pale pigmentation.

results.title: Close Correlation between the First Cleavage Plane and the Body Axis in Early Xenopus Embryos
results.author: Rie Masho1
results.takeaway: The first cleavage plane in early Xenopus embryos is closely related to the body axis, with most of it lying within 30 degrees of the dorsal midline at the initial gastrula stage.

results.title: The dorsoventral axis is specified prior to first cleavage in the direct developing sea urchin Heliocidaris erythrogramma.
results.author: J. J. Henry
results.takeaway: The dorsoventral axis is specified prior to first cleavage in the direct developing sea urchin Heliocidaris erythrogramma, suggesting it is set up in the unfertilized oocyte rather than being set up after fertilization.

results.title: The establishment of embryonic axial properties in the nemertean, Cerebratulus lacteus.
results.author: J. Henry
results.takeaway: The first cleavage division in Cerebratulus lacteus embryos does not play a causal role in establishing the dorsoventral and bilateral axes, suggesting the dorsoventral axis is specified prior to this division.

results.title: Evolutionary dissociation between cleavage, cell lineage and embryonic axes in sea urchin embryos.
results.author: J. J. Henry
results.takeaway: Evolutionary differences in sea urchin embryos show that cell determination is not tied to a single cleavage pattern, but rather to an underlying framework of axial systems.

results.title: The first cleavage plane and the embryonic axis are determined by separate mechanisms in Xenopus laevis. II. Experimental dissociation by lateral compression of the egg.
results.author: S. Black
results.takeaway: Subcortical rotation is the crucial early step in determining the embryonic axis in Xenopus laevis, while the first cleavage plane and the embryonic axis are determined by separate mechanisms.

results.title: Determination of dorso-ventral axis in early embryos of the sea urchin, Hemicentrotus pulcherrimus.
results.author: T. Kominami
results.takeaway: The dorso-ventral axis of sea urchin embryos is established by the spatial arrangement of blastomeres during the 5-6th cleavage stages, with intercellular communication playing a role in blastomere fate during 7-8th cleavage stages.

results.title: Occurrence of dorsal axis-inducing activity around the vegetal pole of an uncleaved Xenopus egg and displacement to the equatorial region by cortical rotation.
results.author: M. Fujisue
results.takeaway: Cortical rotation in amphibian embryogenesis leads to the displacement of dorsal axis-inducing activity from the vegetal pole to the equatorial region, resulting in dorsal specification.

results.title: The first cleavage plane and the embryonic axis are determined by separate mechanisms in Xenopus laevis. I. Independence in undisturbed embryos.
results.author: Michael V. Danilchik
results.takeaway: The first cleavage plane and embryonic axis are determined by separate mechanisms in Xenopus laevis, with the first cleavage plane often not congruent, but convergence and extension during gastrulation and neurulation can cause apparent congruence in half-labeled embryos.

results.title: The establishment of bilateral asymmetry in sea urchin embryos
results.takeaway: The establishment of bilateral asymmetry in sea urchin embryos is dependent on the specification of the dorsoventral axis, which determines the position of the echinus or adult rudiment in the larva.

results.title: THE POINT OF ENTRANCE OF THE SPERMATOZOöN IN RELATION TO THE ORIENTATION OF THE EMBRYO IN EGGS WITH SPIRAL CLEAVAGE
results.author: T. Morgan
results.takeaway: The side of the egg on which the sperm enters may influence the orientation of the embryo in spiral-cleaved eggs, with the plane of cleavage passing to the side on all occasions.

results.title: Timing of Early Developmental Events in Embryos of a Tropical Sea Urchin Echinometra mathaei
results.author: T. Kominami
results.takeaway: The timing of early developmental events in Echinometra mathaei sea urchin embryos is influenced by factors other than cleavage cycles, such as the nucleo-cytoplasmic ratio.

results.title: Implications for dorsoventral axis determination from the zebrafish mutation janus
results.author: S. Abdelilah
results.takeaway: The zebrafish mutation janus suggests that the dorsoventral axis and first cleavage planes are determined by separate mechanisms in the embryo.

results.title: Conserved mechanism of dorsoventral axis determination in equal-cleaving spiralians.
results.author: J. Henry
results.takeaway: The conserved mechanism of cell and axis determination has been conserved among equal-cleaving spiralians, with any cell quadrant becoming the dorsal cell quadrant after the eight-cell stage.

results.title: Localized axis determinant in the early cleavage embryo of the goldfish, Carassius auratus
results.author: Toshiro Mizuno
results.takeaway: The vegetal yolk hemisphere of early cleavage goldfish embryos contains axis determination factors, which are necessary for the generation of dorsal structures, and may be distributed asymmetrically within this hemisphere.

results.title: Generic Theoretical Models to Predict Division Patterns of Cleaving Embryos
results.author: Anaëlle Pierre
results.takeaway: Our 3D-modeling framework accurately predicts cleavage patterns in embryos of fishes, amphibians, echinoderms, and ascidians, revealing the default self-organization rules governing early embryogenesis.

results.title: Establishment of the dorsoventral axis in nemertean embryos: Evolutionary considerations of spiralian development
results.author: J. Henry
results.takeaway: Nemertean embryos show a unique development pattern, with four cell quadrants contributing equally to the formation of dorsal or ventral ectoderm, unlike other spiralian phyla.

results.title: Early Events in Frog Blastomere Fate Determination
results.author: S. Sullivan
results.takeaway: The position of a blastomere during early cleavage stages determines whether its descendants will contribute to the retina, and is crucial for retinal cell specification.

results.title: The frog prince-ss: a molecular formula for dorsoventral patterning in Xenopus.
results.author: Hazel Sive
results.takeaway: The dorsoventral patterning in Xenopus begins early in fertilization, with factors like Wnt proteins, Noggin, and activins playing key roles in forming mesodermal tissues.

results.title: Lineage tracing shows that cell size asymmetries predict the dorsoventral axis in the sea star embryo
results.author: Vanessa Barone
results.takeaway: Cell size asymmetries in sea star embryos predict the dorsoventral axis but are not necessary for directing its formation.

results.title: Subcortical rotation in Xenopus eggs: an early step in embryonic axis specification.
results.author: Jean-Paul Vincent
results.takeaway: Subcortical rotation in Xenopus eggs plays a crucial role in embryonic axis specification, with the amount of rotation correlating to the extent of dorsal development.

results.title: Body axis determination during early development in amphibians.
results.author: P. Savard
results.journal: Biochemistry and cell biology = Biochimie et biologie cellulaire
results.takeaway: Cell communication plays a crucial role in establishing body axes in amphibians, with retinoic acid potentially playing a posteriorizing role.

results.title: Confocal microscopy analysis of living Xenopus eggs and the mechanism of cortical rotation.
results.author: C. Larabell
results.takeaway: Cortical rotation in amphibian embryos occurs before the formation of a detectable parallel array, and motor molecules may be distributed throughout the parallel array, potentially causing microtubules to slide along other microtubules like cilia and eukaryotic flagella.

results.title: Cortical rotation of the Xenopus egg: consequences for the anteroposterior pattern of embryonic dorsal development.
results.author: J. Gerhart
results.takeaway: Reduced cortical rotation in Xenopus eggs leads to incomplete axial pattern development, with body axes deleted for dorsal structures at the anterior end.

results.title: Determinants of early amphibian development
results.author: J. Gerhart
results.takeaway: The direction of egg cortex rotation, influenced by sperm entry, determines the development of anatomical structures in the dorsal midline of amphibian embryos.

results.title: Move it or lose it: axis specification in Xenopus
results.author: C. Weaver
results.takeaway: Cortical rotation and active particle transport cooperate to establish the dorsoventral axis in amphibians, with cortical rotation aligning microtubules and orienting particle transport.

results.title: A step in embryonic axis specification in Xenopus laevis is simulated by cytoplasmic displacements elicited by gravity and centrifugal force.
results.author: Steven D. Black
results.journal: Advances in space research : the official journal of the Committee on Space Research
results.takeaway: Subcortical rotation is crucial for Xenopus embryonic axis determination, and its function should be normal in microgravity.

results.title: Kinematics of gray crescent formation in Xenopus eggs: the displacement of subcortical cytoplasm relative to the egg surface.
results.author: Jean-Paul Vincent
results.takeaway: The rotation of subcortical cytoplasm in Xenopus eggs, rather than convergence, specifies the embryo's prospective axis, resembling the Rana egg's process.

results.title: Gravitational effects on the rearrangement of cytoplasmic components during axial formation in amphibian development.
results.author: C. Phillips
results.journal: Advances in space research : the official journal of the Committee on Space Research
results.takeaway: Gravitational effects on amphibian development influence the rearrangement of cytoplasmic components, which may play a role in establishing the dorsal-ventral axis.

results.title: Microgravity simulation as a probe for understanding early Xenopus pattern specification.
results.author: A. Neff
results.journal: Journal of embryology and experimental morphology
results.takeaway: Microgravity simulation shows that normal development of amphibian eggs in space should be possible, challenging models that rely on gravity-driven rearrangements for cytoplasmic organization.

results.title: Axis formation in annual killifish: Nodal and β-catenin regulate morphogenesis without Huluwa prepatterning
results.author: Philip B. Abitua
results.takeaway: Nodal and -catenin signaling play a crucial role in regulating morphogenesis in annual killifish embryogenesis, avoiding the need for Huluwa prepatterning.

results.title: Massive cytoplasmic transport and microtubule organization in fertilized chordate eggs.
results.author: T. Nishikata
results.takeaway: Chordate eggs show cortical rotation and a parallel microtubule array during early development, with the noncentrosomal MT organizing center playing a key role in determining the embryonic axis.

results.title: A transient array of parallel microtubules in frog eggs: potential tracks for a cytoplasmic rotation that specifies the dorso-ventral axis.
results.author: R. Elinson
results.takeaway: Parallel microtubules in frog eggs serve as tracks for the cortical/cytoplasmic rotation, which specifies the dorsoventral axis of the embryo.

results.title: Early development of cephalochordates (amphioxus)
results.author: L. Holland
results.takeaway: Cephalochordates (amphioxus) develop through a complex patterning mechanism involving secreted signaling proteins, similar to vertebrates.

results.title: Patterns of microtubule polymerization relating to cortical rotation in Xenopus laevis eggs.
results.author: E. Houliston
results.takeaway: The cortical microtubule array in Xenopus eggs rotates with the cytoplasm, forming as a result of asymmetric microtubule growth outward from cytoplasm to cortex.

results.title: Evolution of Axis Specification Mechanisms in Jawed Vertebrates: Insights from a Chondrichthyan
results.author: M. Coolen
results.takeaway: The dogfish shows similarities with amniotes in embryonic axis specification, suggesting an evolutionary link between dorso-ventral polarity and embryonic-extraembryonic organization in gnathostomes.

results.title: Amphibian egg cytoplasm response to altered g-forces and gravity orientation.
results.author: A. W. Neff
results.journal: Advances in space research : the official journal of the Committee on Space Research
results.takeaway: Amphibian egg cytoplasm is compartmentalized, and altered gravity orientation and gravity force affect cytoplasmic compartment rearrangements, affecting embryonic development.

results.title: Twinning of amphibian embryos by centrifugation
results.author: S. Black
results.takeaway: Centrifugation of amphibian eggs can produce conjoined twins with two sets of axial structures, regardless of the position of sperm entry.

results.title: Specification of embryonic axis and mosaic development in ascidians
results.author: H. Nishida
results.takeaway: Maternal factors and cell interactions play crucial roles in specifying embryonic axes and cell fates in ascidians, with beta-catenin playing a crucial role in vegetal development.

145.state: The relationship Przibram probed is largely settled: in Xenopus the first cleavage furrow tends to demarcate the dorsoventral axis (within ~30 degrees), with the axis set by cortical rotation before cleavage — though the coupling is statistical and varies across species. Cytoplasmic and molecular determinants superseded the marking approach.

facets.q: How do starvation and caloric restriction affect growth, body size, and subsequent regeneration?
What are the effects of nutrient deprivation on developmental growth and recovery?

results.title: The cellular basis of feeding-dependent body size plasticity in sea anemones
results.author: Kathrin Garschall
results.takeaway: Feeding-dependent body size plasticity in sea anemones is influenced by cellular changes, with TOR signaling playing a key role in polyp growth and cell proliferation during feeding.

results.title: SAT-201 Effects Of Early Postnatal Undernutrition On Growth Plate Chondrocyte Gene Expression
results.author: Kirtal Hansdah
results.takeaway: Early postnatal undernutrition impairs growth plate chondrogenesis and cell survival, potentially contributing to short stature due to malnutrition.

results.title: Effect of caloric restriction on organ size and its contribution to metabolic adaptation: an ancillary analysis of CALERIE 2
results.author: K. Falkenhain
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction leads to reduced adipose tissue and skeletal muscle mass, and persistent metabolic adaptation, with advanced imaging techniques potentially providing insights into organ-specific contributions to energy metabolism.

results.title: The Effects of Graded Levels of Calorie Restriction: XVIII.Tissue-Specific Changes in Cell Size and Number in Response to Calorie Restriction
results.author: Daniel Phillips
results.journal: The Journals of Gerontology Series A: Biological Sciences and Medical Sciences
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction leads to tissue-specific changes in cell size and number, with white adipose tissue being used for energy and brown adipose tissue being used for heat regulation.

results.title: Cellular response in rats during malnutrition at various ages.
results.author: M. Winick
results.takeaway: Malnutrition at various ages in rats affects cell size and growth, with early malnutrition impairing cell division and later malnutrition resulting in cell size reduction, which can be recovered on refeeding.

results.title: Effects of starvation and refeeding on growth performance, appetite, growth hormone–insulin-like growth factor axis levels and digestive function of Acipenser dabryanus
results.author: Xiaoyun Wu
results.takeaway: Short-term fasting followed by refeeding in Acipenser dabryanus fish results in full compensation, with the lowest physiological and biochemical effects occurring after 3 days of starvation and 14 days of refeeding.

results.title: Effect of 2-year caloric restriction on organ and tissue size in nonobese 21- to 50-year-old adults in a randomized clinical trial: the CALERIE study.
results.author: W. Shen
results.journal: The American journal of clinical nutrition
results.takeaway: Two years of caloric restriction in nonobese young adults led to preferential loss of adipose tissue, particularly VAT, over muscle and organ tissue loss.

results.title: Caloric restriction leads to high marrow adiposity and low bone mass in growing mice
results.author: M. Devlin
results.journal: Journal of bone and mineral research : the official journal of the American Society for Bone and Mineral Research
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction during rapid skeletal growth in young mice leads to impaired bone acquisition, low leptin and IGF-1 levels, and high marrow adiposity.

results.title: The effects of graded levels of calorie restriction: I. impact of short term calorie and protein restriction on body composition in the C57BL/6 mouse
results.author: S. Mitchell
results.takeaway: Short-term calorie restriction in C57BL/6 mice leads to differential utilization of body tissues, with adipose tissue depots being the most significant source of energy, and small changes in vital organs.

results.title: Long-Term Hyperphagia and Caloric Restriction Caused by Low- or High-Density Husbandry Have Differential Effects on Zebrafish Postembryonic Development, Somatic Growth, Fat Accumulation and Reproduction
results.author: S. Leibold
results.takeaway: Long-term caloric restriction in zebrafish leads to severe obesity, with aging-associated anorexic effects, while juvenile fish remain resistant to obesity and somatic growth prioritized in sexually mature adults.

results.title: Effects of Starvation and Refeeding on Growth, Digestion, Nonspecific Immunity and Lipid-Metabolism-Related Genes in Onychostoma macrolepis
results.author: Nina Gou
results.journal: Animals : an Open Access Journal from MDPI
results.takeaway: Starvation and refeeding in Onychostoma macrolepis fish lead to reduced growth, improved immunity, and lipid metabolic adaptation, with potential implications for large-scale culturing and release.

results.title: Transgenerational Effects of Early Life Starvation on Growth, Reproduction, and Stress Resistance in Caenorhabditis elegans
results.author: Meghan A. Jobson
results.takeaway: Early life starvation in Caenorhabditis elegans leads to slower growth, reduced fecundity, and increased stress resistance in the exposed animals and their descendants, with some effects potentially adaptive.

results.title: Food deprivation and refeeding influence growth, nutrient retention and functional recovery of rats.
results.author: Julio J. Boza
results.takeaway: Starvation in rats leads to lower muscle glutamine levels and impaired intestinal mucosa, but refeeding rapidly repairs gut atrophy and normalizes intestinal permeability.

results.title: Leptin reverses the inhibitory effect of caloric restriction on longitudinal growth.
results.author: G. Gat-Yablonski
results.takeaway: Leptin stimulates growth even in the presence of caloric restriction, despite reduced serum IGF-I levels, independently of peripheral IGF-I.

results.title: The effect of calorie restriction on mouse skeletal muscle is sex, strain and time-dependent
results.author: L. Boldrin
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction can increase muscle satellite cell number and collagen VI content, but delays regenerative response to injury, with effects varying between mouse strains and sexes.

results.title: Calorie restriction and calorie dilution have different impacts on body fat, metabolism, behavior, and hypothalamic gene expression.
results.author: Xue Liu
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction (CR) leads to increased hunger and better metabolic health compared to calorie dilution (CD), with hunger signaling playing a key role in the benefits of CR.

results.title: Caloric restriction as a mechanism mediating resistance to environmental disease.
results.author: L. Frame
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction in rodents leads to improved defense against environmental stress, potentially explaining the protective effects of low body weight on radiation and chemically induced cancers.

results.title: Honoring Clive McCay and 75 years of calorie restriction research.
results.author: R. McDonald
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction extends lifespan in various species, including humans, by reducing growth and preventing premature death during development.

results.title: Effects of graded calorie restriction: XXII. Impact of long-term graded calorie restriction on tissue partitioning, digestive efficiency, bone health, and coordination in male C57BL/6J mice
results.author: Sharon E Mitchell
results.journal: The Journals of Gerontology Series A: Biological Sciences and Medical Sciences
results.takeaway: Long-term graded calorie restriction in male C57BL/6J mice improves tissue utilization, digestive efficiency, bone health, and motor coordination, with favorable effects on bone health contradicting previous negative reports.

results.title: Caloric restriction.
results.author: J. Speakman
results.takeaway: Caloric restriction extends life and reduces age-related diseases in animals, but has negative side effects and is not without challenges.

results.title: Nutrition and brain development in early life.
results.author: Elizabeth L. Prado
results.takeaway: Early nutrient deficiency during pregnancy and infancy can impair brain development, but factors like environment, timing, and recovery play a role in long-term cognitive deficits.

results.title: The impact of undernutrition and overnutrition on early brain development.
results.author: Pauline Samia
results.takeaway: Undernutrition and overnutrition negatively impact early brain development, affecting cognition, emotional regulation, and long-term mental health.

results.title: Dual role of starvation signaling in promoting growth and recovery
results.author: Yonat Gurvich
results.takeaway: Cells subject to nutrient depletion prepare for growth in limiting conditions and for their predicted recovery when nutrients are replenished.

results.title: Nutrient Deprivation in Artemia franciscana: Developmental Stage, Nutritional History, and Phenotypes Linked to Conserved Pathways
results.author: Nikola Mitović
results.takeaway: Starvation responses in Artemia franciscana are shaped by developmental stage and prior nutritional history, with higher mortality in adults and energy-conserving behavioral adaptations.

results.title: Nutrition and the developing brain: nutrient priorities and measurement.
results.author: M. Georgieff
results.journal: The American journal of clinical nutrition
results.takeaway: Nutrients and growth factors regulate brain development during fetal and early postnatal life, with specific nutrients having greater effects on brain development than others.

results.title: Dietary nutrient-induced quiescence delays neural stem cell proliferation in the Drosophila central brain
results.author: Mary Buktaw
results.journal: Proceedings of the West Virginia Academy of Science
results.takeaway: Dietary nutrient deprivation in larvae can temporarily delay neural stem cell proliferation in the central brain, but these effects are temporary and neuroblasts return to normal proliferative behavior after more recovery time.

results.title: Adaptive Responses in Severe Acute Malnutrition: Endocrinology, Metabolomics, Mortality, and Growth
results.author: Laura Page
results.takeaway: Children with severe acute malnutrition adapt by maintaining essential bodily functions and reducing growth rate, with potential long-term complications.

results.title: Prenatal malnutrition and development of the brain.
results.author: P. Morgane
results.takeaway: Prenatal protein malnutrition negatively impacts brain development, leading to various learning disabilities and affecting attention and environmental interactions.

results.title: IV. Growth Failure in Institutionalized Children
results.author: Dana E. Johnson
results.journal: Monographs of the Society for Research in Child Development
results.takeaway: Children in institutional settings experience significant growth suppression due to nutritional insufficiencies and social deprivation, potentially leading to metabolic syndrome in adulthood.

results.title: Effects of Nutrient-Induced Developmental Delay on Adult Drosophila Brain Structure
results.author: H. White
results.journal: Proceedings of the West Virginia Academy of Science
results.takeaway: Nutrient-induced developmental delay in fruit flies leads to reduced neuroblast proliferation, potentially affecting adult brain structure.

results.title: Can Children Catch up from the Consequences of Undernourishment? Evidence from Child Linear Growth, Developmental Epigenetics, and Brain and Neurocognitive Development
results.author: Jef L. Leroy
results.takeaway: Children can recover from linear growth deficits, but undernutrition early in life may have profound, irreversible effects on brain and neurocognitive development.

results.title: Nutrient-regulated dynamics of chondroprogenitors in the postnatal murine growth plate
results.author: Takeshi Oichi
results.takeaway: Nutrient availability regulates the balance between accumulation and differentiation of chondroprogenitors in the growth plate, with IGF-1 partially mediating this regulation by promoting committed differentiation of chondroprogenitors.

results.title: Impact of Micronutrient Deficiencies on Growth: The Stunting Syndrome
results.author: Francesco Branca
results.takeaway: Stunting syndrome, caused by micronutrient deficiencies, can be prevented and treated through exclusive breastfeeding and adequate micronutrient-rich foods.

results.title: Nutrition in adolescent growth and development.
results.author: S. Norris
results.takeaway: Adolescent nutrition plays a crucial role in shaping adult health and preventing non-communicable diseases, with implications for puberty timing, muscle, fat mass, and risk of non-communicable diseases.

results.title: NUTRITION, GROWTH AND NEUROINTEGRATIVE DEVELOPMENT: AN EXPERIMENTAL AND ECOLOGIC STUDY
results.author: J. Cravioto
results.takeaway: Malnutrition in early childhood may negatively impact neurointegrative functioning, leading to delayed development, poorer learning, and reduced memory retention.

results.title: Is There Complete, Partial, or No Recovery from Childhood Malnutrition? – Empirical Evidence from Indonesia
results.author: Subha Mani
results.journal: Oxford Bulletin of Economics and Statistics
results.takeaway: Childhood malnutrition causes partial recovery in height growth, with younger children, stunted children, and those living in communities with six or more health posts showing larger recovery.

results.title: Effects of maternal malnutrition and postnatal nutritional rehabilitation on brain fatty acids, learning, and memory.
results.author: A. S. de Souza
results.takeaway: Early undernutrition, particularly in developing countries, can negatively impact brain development and function, leading to deficits in spatial cognitive learning ability.

results.title: The Effects of Nutrition on Linear Growth
results.author: E. Inzaghi
results.takeaway: Nutrition plays a crucial role in linear growth, with hormonal signals playing a key role in regulating growth and nutrient intake being crucial for optimal child growth.

134.state: Caloric restriction and undernutrition are intensely studied: they reduce cell number and size, impair growth-plate chondrogenesis, and trigger TOR-mediated size plasticity and persistent metabolic adaptation. Using starvation as a lever on the growth problem is a live physiological theme.

facets.q: Is Pleurodeles waltl an established model organism for limb and organ regeneration?
What makes the Iberian ribbed newt a tractable laboratory model in developmental biology?

results.title: Reading and editing the Pleurodeles waltl genome reveals novel features of tetrapod regeneration
results.author: Heng Wang
results.takeaway: The Pleurodeles waltl genome reveals novel features of tetrapod regeneration, including the co-expression of embryonic stem cell-specific miRNAs and Harbinger DNA transposons during limb regeneration.

results.title: Improved transcriptome assembly and functional annotation of Pleurodeles waltl for regeneration research
results.author: M. Y. Yassouf
results.takeaway: The updated transcriptome assembly for Pleurodeles waltl provides a more comprehensive representation of reads and proteins, enhancing regeneration research and providing valuable insights into molecular mechanisms.

results.title: Ex vivo expansion of primary cells from limb tissue of Pleurodeles waltl
results.author: Mahmudul Hasan
results.takeaway: Fibronectin coating provides the best biocompatible environment for cell outgrowth and attachment in Pleurodeles waltl limb tissue for in vitro experiments.

results.title: Sequencing and chromosome-scale assembly of the giant Pleurodeles waltl genome
results.author: Thomas Brown
results.takeaway: The Pleurodeles waltl genome provides a valuable resource for studying regenerative, developmental, and evolutionary principles, with DNA transposable elements playing a major role in its expansion.

results.title: Molecular genetic system for regenerative studies using newts
results.author: Toshinori Hayashi
results.takeaway: The Iberian ribbed newt (Pleurodeles waltl) is a suitable model animal for studying regeneration using molecular genetics, with comparable abilities to Japanese common newts and a method for efficient transgenesis.

results.title: Chromosome-scale genome assembly reveals how repeat elements shape non-coding RNA landscapes active during newt limb regeneration
results.author: Thomas Brown
results.takeaway: Intronic repeat elements in newt genomes drive non-coding RNA biogenesis and regeneration-specific expression, providing insights into adult regeneration and developmental principles.

results.title: Appendage‐restricted gene induction using a heated agarose gel for studying regeneration in metamorphosed Xenopus laevis and Pleurodeles waltl
results.author: Haruka Matsubara
results.takeaway: The "agarose-embedded heat shock" method allows for precise and reproducible gene induction in amphibian appendages, aiding in understanding molecular mechanisms underlying regeneration.

results.title: Genetic compensation response contributes to Pleurodeles waltl limb regeneration
results.author: Binxu Yin
results.takeaway: Genetic compensation response (GCR) maintains the robustness of limb regeneration in highly regenerative salamanders, allowing Yap knockout without compromising regeneration.

results.title: Cas9 ribonucleoprotein complex allows direct and rapid analysis of coding and noncoding regions of target genes in Pleurodeles waltl development and regeneration.
results.author: Miyuki Suzuki
results.takeaway: Cas9 ribonucleoprotein complex (RNP) enables efficient gene knockout in Pleurodeles waltl, accelerating gene functional analysis in salamanders.

results.title: Insights regarding skin regeneration in non-amniote vertebrates: Skin regeneration without scar formation and potential step-up to a higher level of regeneration.
results.author: Gembu Abe
results.takeaway: Fish, anurans, and urodeles can regenerate skin without scar formation, and their models can help study how skin regeneration can be stepped up to organ regeneration.

results.title: Differentiated muscle cells of salamander Pleurodeles waltl re-enter the cell cycle
results.author: Hao Cai
results.takeaway: This study successfully established a method for isolating and cultivating muscle stem cells from salamanders, providing a valuable tool for exploring molecular mechanisms governing skeletal muscle regeneration.

results.title: A comprehensive reference transcriptome resource for the Iberian ribbed newt Pleurodeles waltl, an emerging model for developmental and regeneration biology
results.author: M. Matsunami
results.journal: DNA Research: An International Journal for Rapid Publication of Reports on Genes and Genomes
results.takeaway: The transcriptome resource for the Iberian ribbed newt Pleurodeles waltl provides a comprehensive genetic resource for regeneration, developmental biology, stem cell biology, and cancer research.

results.title: Co-operative Bmp- and Fgf-signaling inputs convert skin wound healing to limb formation in urodele amphibians.
results.author: Aki Makanae
